title: Confusing & broken Inheritance - Nightly 1.77.2
author: pauldurham
body_snippet:
The items below are incredibly confusing without proper help text, and don't seem to work as expected:
- Inheritance
- Default Properties
- Default inheritance

It is not obvious how to use the above functions.  Also, the default inheritance doesn't appear to work.

## Expected Behavior
The above items should allow the user to set default properties & inheritance and then turn on inheritance, but they don't seems to work properly.  This is assuming I am even configuring the settings corr...

iterations.description: The inheritance mechanism is described as 'confusing and broken', implying a functional defect that needs to be addressed for correct and predictable behavior.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: I completely agree with this point, we already had a discussion about the inheritance in regards with the Clipboard Settings in Issue #1968.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The inheritance mechanism is described as 'confusing and broken', implying a functional defect that needs to be addressed for correct and predictable behavior.
Approach: Debug the existing inheritance logic within `ConnectionInfo.cs` and `ConnectionInfoInheritance.cs` to understand the 'confusing and broken' aspects. Reproduce reported issues, then implement a fix to ensure that properties are inherited correctly and predictably. This will likely involve reviewing property propagation, null/default value handling, and potentially how parent/child relationships are resolved. Ensure UI components (`ConnectionInfoPropertyGrid.cs`, `ConfigWindow.cs`) correctly display and allow configuration of the refined inheritance.
